title: Lethedon cernua
taxonomy: Lethedon cernua is classified within the family Gentianaceae. This family of flowering plants includes approximately 90 genera and over 1,700 species. Lethedon cernua is typically placed in the subfamily Gentianoideae. Further classification includes its order Gentianales and division Magnoliophyta. The classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.

intro: Lethedon cernua, also known as Lethedon, is a plant species belonging to the family Menispermaceae, which is a genus of flowering plants native to tropical regions. The health benefits of Lethedon cernua include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and act as an antiseptic agent, making it useful for treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lethedon cernua include alkaloids, such as lathyranine, lathyrine, and N-oxides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lethedon cernua include tinctures, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
